page: Amazon Web Services m8gn.metal-24xl is a bare-metal, general-purpose server instance powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Utilizing the arm64 architecture, this server delivers 96 dedicated physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz. With a single thread per core and dedicated CPU allocation, the hardware eliminates resource contention and virtualization overhead, ensuring consistent compute performance for multi-threaded applications.

The instance is configured with 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced ratio of 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core. It does not feature local storage, requiring all storage operations to utilize network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. To support intensive storage and data transfer demands, the server provides a baseline network bandwidth of 300 Gbps, which is optimized for EBS connectivity and high-throughput network communication.

This hardware configuration is designed for workloads that benefit from high-density Arm compute and substantial network throughput. It is highly suitable for distributed databases, containerized microservices, large-scale CI/CD pipelines, and web serving. The bare-metal architecture combined with the energy-efficient Graviton design offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for modern cloud-native architectures.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.metal-24xl is a general-purpose, bare-metal server powered by AWS Graviton arm64 processors. It features 96 dedicated physical cores running at 2.8 GHz with a 1:1 thread-to-core ratio, providing predictable compute performance. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, translating to 4.0 GB per core, but does not include local storage, relying instead on network-attached EBS volumes. A key hardware highlight is its high-speed networking, delivering a baseline bandwidth of 300 Gbps, which is highly optimized for EBS and data-intensive network traffic. This hardware profile offers excellent resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for Arm-native deployments. It is well-suited for high-throughput network applications, distributed databases, containerized microservices, and large-scale CI/CD pipelines.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gn.metal-24xl is a general-purpose bare-metal server featuring 96 dedicated arm64 Graviton cores, 384 GB of RAM, and 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput workloads.

tagline: A high-bandwidth, bare-metal arm64 instance featuring ninety-six dedicated cores and three hundred gigabits per second of baseline network throughput.
bullet_points: Ninety-six dedicated AWS Graviton arm64 physical cores running at 2.8 GHz
384.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
High-speed 300 Gbps baseline network bandwidth optimized for EBS
Bare-metal deployment with no virtualization overhead
Designed for distributed databases, containerized microservices, and CI/CD
